title: python cleanup
body:
Description
---------------

`lib/core/dictionary.py` -> fix typo, remove redundant parenthesis since you are returning a tuple anyway
`lib/core/structures.py` -> remove redundant class parenthesis (not needed in python3)
`lib/reports/html_report.py` -> simplify comparison statements

step_plan.summary: Clean up Python syntax/style across three modules by removing redundant parentheses in tuple returns, dropping empty class parentheses for Python 3 compatibility, and simplifying HTML report status checks.

steps.operation: Refactor __getstate__ to return a tuple without extra parentheses
steps.reason: Remove redundant parentheses around a tuple return to align with Python idioms while preserving the exact data returned by __getstate__.
steps.side_effects: No functional change; ensure unpickling continues to produce the same tuple; may require running tests related to pickling

steps.operation: Remove redundant class parentheses in Python 3 style
steps.reason: Drop empty parentheses in class declaration to adhere to Python 3 style without changing behavior.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change; downstream imports unaffected; minor impact on readability

steps.operation: Simplify status code comparisons in HTMLReport.generate using range checks
steps.reason: Replace chained comparisons with range-based conditions to improve clarity while preserving equivalent logic for status evaluation.
steps.side_effects: No API change; ensure test coverage for HTML report remains valid; potential minor changes in readability for maintainers
